briefdescription.para: The following constants map to the XPAR parameters created in the xparameters.h file.

detaileddescription.para: They are defined here such that a user can easily change all the needed parameters in one place.

briefdescription.para: The following constant defines the address of the IIC temperature sensor device on the IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: Note that since the address is only 7 bits, this constant is the address divided by 2.

briefdescription.para: Maximum delay count used for timeout handling.
The instance of the IIC device.

detaileddescription.para: The following structure contains fields that are used with the callbacks (handlers) of the IIC driver. The driver asynchronously calls handlers when abnormal events occur or when data has been sent or received. This structure must be volatile to work when the code is optimized.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the temperature of the IIC temperature sensor on the IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: It initializes the IIC device driver and sets it up to communicate with the temperature sensor. This function does contain a loop that polls for completion of the IIC processing such that it may not return if interrupts or the hardware are not working.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device
is the address of the Temperature Sensor device on the IIC bus
is the data byte read from the temperature sensor

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS to indicate success, else XST_FAILURE to indicate a Failure.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This receive handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and and indicates that data in the specified buffer was received.

detaileddescription.para: The byte count should equal the byte count of the buffer if all the buffer was filled.

CallBackRef
is a pointer to the IIC device driver instance which the handler is being called for.
ByteCount
indicates the number of bytes remaining to be received of the requested byte count. A value of zero indicates all requested bytes were received.
None. None.

briefdescription.para: This status handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and indicates that the conditions of the IIC bus changed.

detaileddescription.para: This handler should not be called for the application unless an error occurs.

CallBackRef
is a pointer to the IIC device driver instance which the handler is being called for.
Status
contains the status of the IIC bus which changed.
None. None.

briefdescription.para: The purpose of this function is to illustrate how to use the IIC driver to read the temperature.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, XST_FAILURE if unsuccessful

briefdescription.para: This file contains an interrupt based design example which uses the Xilinx IIC device and driver to exercise the temperature sensor on the ML300 board.

detaileddescription.para: This example only performs read operations (receive) from the IIC temperature sensor of the platform.
The XIic_MasterRecv() API is used to receive the data.
This example assumes that there is an interrupt controller in the hardware system and the IIC device is connected to the interrupt controller.
7-bit addressing is used to access the tempsensor.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jhl 09/10/03 Created 1.00a sv 05/09/05 Minor changes to comply to Doxygen and coding guidelines 3.4 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. 3.10 gm 07/09/23 Added SDT support. 3.14 bkv 07/07/25 Fixed GCC Warnings. 3.15 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details. 3.15 vlt 02/17/26 Added timeout handling to polled wait loops.
